Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Queers for Economic Justice records
Queers for Economic Justice
7802
13 cubic feet. 13 cubic feet.
5 mapcase folders. 5 mapcase folders.
1 items. 1 items
circa 2000-2014. Surveys, posters, banners, and records of the organization. The records of QEJ consist of key documentation of its founding goals and guiding principles, its sources of funding, the involvement of its staff, executive directors, and Board of Directors, its publicity and outreach efforts, and its major projects including the establishment and operation of a shelter for queer people facing homelessness. Together, these files give a detailed look at the ideas, actions, challenges, and successes of this innovative organization devoted to racial, economic, gender, and sexual equality over its dozen years of operation. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 1 14 (unprocessed) ======= 1 14 (unprocessed) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 8628388
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
July 2016
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, July 2016
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, April 2017
ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
Formed in 2002 in New York City, Queers for Economic Justice (QEJ) was a "progressive non-profit organization committed to promoting economic justice in a context of sexual and gender liberation." As described on its website, its goal was "to challenge and change the systems that create poverty and economic injustice in our communities, and to promote an economic system that embraces sexual and gender diversity." Distinctive among LGBTQI groups at the time in its foregrounding of poverty and class, QEJ introduced different solutions to the national discussion of the queer agenda. On the local level, it spearheaded changes in New York City laws and policies affecting homeless transgender people and gave tangible assistance to the City's queer people in poverty. Its work policies and staff reflected its commitment to being "multi-racial, multi-classed, multi-cultural group of people of diverse marginalized sexual and gender identities, as well as diverse ages, skills, educational levels, backgrounds and abilities."
In 2006, while substantial political effort focused on attaining marriage equality, QEJ brought together twenty-two activists who crafted a report, later signed by hundreds, that pushed for a wider vision. The report, titled Beyond Same-Sex Marriage: A New Strategic Vision For All Our Families and Relationships, became known as the "Beyond Marriage statement." Over time, QEJ published reports on a variety of topics at the intersection of gender, sexuality, and economic justice, offering perspectives on homelessness and housing, economic justice, immigration, drug laws and public health, marriage and other family forms, and welfare. QEJ's major initiatives included community organizing, research and policy development, leadership training, education, outreach, legal aid and advocacy, and community support groups.
Joseph DeFilippis was QEJ's founding executive director. Subsequent leaders were Kenyon Farrow (2009-2011) and Amber Hollibaugh (2011-2014). Jay Toole served as QEJ's founding and only Shelter Director, leading the organization's work relating to housing and homelessness. Among the other LGBTQ, anti-racism, and anti-poverty activists and scholars engaged with the work of QEJ were Ricky Blum Terry Boggis, Ann Cammett, Aine Duggan, Lisa Duggan, Kenyon Farrow, Monroe France, Felix Gardon, Reina Gossett, Richard Kim, Maureen Lane, Ignacio Rivera, Dean Spade, and Jessica Stern.
Due to financial difficulties, QEJ closed in 2014. The organization's projects in the areas of housing and work continue through Jay's House, a shelter non-profit organization in Brooklyn
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
The records of QEJ consist of key documentation of its founding goals and guiding principles, its sources of funding, the involvement of its staff, executive directors, and Board of Directors, its publicity and outreach efforts, and its major projects including the establishment and operation of a shelter for queer people facing homelessness. Together, these files give a detailed look at the ideas, actions, challenges, and successes of this innovative organization devoted to racial, economic, gender, and sexual equality over its dozen years of operation.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Foundation of Organization, Bylaws, and Constitution Box 2-3
Series II. Grants and Foundation Files Box 1-2, 13
Series III. Board Files Box 7-8, 11-13
Series IV. Financial Records Box 10
Series V. Project Files Box 3-6, 10, 13
Series VI. Welfare Rights and Shelter Director Box 8-9, 13
Series VII. Students, Volunteer, and Intern Program Box 9
Series VIII. Publicity Box 7, Mapcase folders
Series IX. Publications, Packets, and General Information Box 9-10
Cite As:
Queers for Economic Justice records, #7802. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Access Restrictions:
Access to Boxes 11 and 12 restricted until 2091.
Transgender people -- Economic conditions. Transgender people -- Legal status, laws, etc. Transsexuals -- United States -- Economic conditions. Bisexuals -- United States -- Economic conditions. Lesbians -- United States -- Economic conditions. Gays -- United States -- Economic conditions. Gay liberation movement -- United States. Queers for Economic Justice
8628388 <<<<<<< HEAD Series I. Foundation of Organization, Bylaws, and Constitution
Scope and Contents
This series contains the QEJ personnel manual and other items that reveal how the organization operated internally as well as its goals and mission.Certificate of Incorporation2004221Charitable Choice Forum251QEJ Personnel manual and surveys252Manhattan Neighborhood Network grant material2008253Training254QUISGLEYA2000255HR Benefits2007256Welfare Made a Difference2000257501 (c) 32004258Meeting Minutes and NotesJanuary-October2000259-68QEJ retreat201131Astraea201132Personnel Manual33Grant Letters201034Health Services201235Third Wave Foundation200936Paul Rapoport Foundation201137QEJ Executive Director Search201138Grants and Donations39Search Committee2011310Astraea2012311-12Mertz-Gilmore2011313Stonewall2010314QEJ New Funding Possibilities315Arcus2011316New York Women's Foundation2011317New York Community Trust2011318New York City HIV Planning Group2012319Calamus2008320New York Women's Foundation Grantee Meeting2010321Rapoport Fund2011322Union square2012323DPA Proposal2010-2011324Rapoport2012323QEJ Contact info, Minutes, Structures (from binder)2003324-25QEJ Surveys, OSI, Pride '03, Cause Effective, Banner, Name/Logo (from binder)2003326Agendas and Minutes, Mission (from binder)2003327Binder, "Know Your Rights"3Binder, "Raise The Money You Need: Building Your Individual Donor Program"20063QEJ History75Building a Queer Left2000-200476Series II. Grants and Foundation Files
Scope and Contents
QEJ obtained funding from numerous sources, including the Astraea Lesbian Foundation, Ben and Jerry's, Gill Foundation, Jewish Fund for Justice, New York Community Trust, New York Women's Foundation, Stonewall Foundation, Unitarian Universalists, and the Paul Rapoport Foundation whose records are preserved at Cornell.DOH Reimbursements201011Union Square201112Open Society Institute (OSI)201113Public Welfare Foundation2003-201014Paul Rapoport2005-201015Mary Gryton16

Board Files
Scope and Contents
Board agendas and minutes give top-level insight into the plans and operations of an organization. Conditions Governing Access
Access restrictedSeries IV. Financial Records
Scope and Contents
Budget materials are in this series, along with miscellaneous files that might rightfully belong in other series, including speeches, conferences, and information on programs and an affordable housing rally.Social Security City Council Hearing2005107Philadelphia Bar Association2005108Conferences, speeches109Domestic Partners in Shelters1010Affordable Housing Rally1011Election Town Hall Meeting20041012Program information2006-20111013CORI/Bookkeepers20101014Landlord dispute20131015N. Cheng20111016Audits2008-20091017-18Mailing list1019Lease20121020Lease and consultant agreement, receipt2010-20111021Budget notes, meeting minutes, tax information2005-20061022NYS Department of Labor20041023Income reports2004-20051024Income and expense reports2004-20051025Budget20041026Budget20061027Audit20041028WWRC Resource Guide1029IRS20031030Sierra admin job description1031Lila interview about LGBT aging1032YWCA/QEJ coalition1033Building meeting20111034Pride at Work20101035Sex worker statements20111036Phone banking document and lists20131037-38QEJ projects1039Black and Pink agreement20121040Faith and Labor Project1041FIERCE/Building incident20111042Whitehouse conference on Housing and Astraea forms20121043Rapoport building work1044Correspondence on blog posts, copies of articles20061045QEJ Network: DYCD1046HIV Ban20071047Series V. Project Files
Scope and Contents
These files document the core work QEJ did, including sharing their insight and knowledge at Creating Change, the annual activist conference hosted by the National LGBT Task Force. There is good documentation of the annual Amazingly Queer Race for Economic Justice, a "scavenger hunt across the city, in which competing teams will raise funds to combat poverty, homelessness and other urban injustices." There are three folders about the working group that wrote Beyond Same-Sex Marriage: A New Strategic Vision For All Our Families and Relationships, and these shed light on how the marriage summit came to be and the behind-the-scenes thinking about it.Pages from meeting brainstorming328Shelter Safety Project329Sheets from a workshop QEJ convened2001330Class and Queer Agenda Making2002331Creating Change Conference and Class Institute docs2004332Welfare Queens333Institute334Corporate Funding2004335Group Feedback2005336The Amazingly Queer Race clues and information2012337Amazing Race for Economic Justice338Corporate Welfare project sheets339Creating Change2005340Institute2003341General C.C. Students, Volunteer, and Intern Program
Scope and Contents
Among the job descriptions and reports about students, volunteers, and interns are information about a QEJ transgender meeting, and notes from a 1999 QEJ network meeting, prior to QEJ's founding.Grant information2008-2011912QEJ trans meeting information913Legal Aid Society2006914NGLTF Consultant Agreements915Summer intern report2012916Volunteer forms917Homelessness forum918Amazing Queer Race sign-in2011919Shelter Facilitator training materials2013920QEJ Supervision921JT Education plan922QEJ Staff Meeting2010923Doyin2011924Caterers925Reina2011926Job descriptions, SEED Report2011927First public work and meeting for QEJ network1999928QEJ supervision report2004930Doyin supervision931Debanuj supervision2006-2007932Shelter organization933Travel/trips2008934Volunteer forms135Series VIII. Publicity
Scope and Contents
The brochures, fliers, booklets, and posters designed to share information about the Shelter Safety Welfare Warriors, the Amazingly Queer Race for Economic Justice, and other projects, as well as articles about QEJ and one folder on QEJ's history. The folder "Why Welfare Reform is a Queer Issue" includes materials from 1996-1999, prior to QEJ's founding. Among the posters are:"Miss Jay Toole: Building for Social Justice," "No Human is Illegal," "Sex Worker Visions," "End Eviction Orders!" "Welfare Made a Difference," handmade signs and posters, and a plastic QEJ sign.Pamphlets, fliers, and booklets71-2Amazingly Queer Race planning book (from binder)201173QEJ Support Group Feedback2004-200774QEJ History75Building a Queer Left2000-200476Amazingly Queer Race folder packet77Why Welfare Reform is a Queer Issue1996-199978Welfare Reform1996-199979City Limits article2001710Speaking About Economic Justice2000711Social Security Panel: Creating Change2005712DASIS Letter2001713Planet Out article2000714QEJ stickers and cards715Welfare Warriors research collaborative716Shelter Safety Welfare Warriors717WWRC Attendance2008718WWRC information2007-2009719Articles20012004720"Welfare Made a Difference" and "Gay City: Vol. 3 No. 319" posters20041"They take you in the morning, they will be coming for us at night" poster1"Tales of the Waria" movie poster1Miss Jay Toole: Building for Social Justice poster1"No Human is Illegal" poster1Council of the City of New York proclamation to QEJ1Two vinyl banners banners ("Queers for Economic Justice," carried in marches and parades; "Beyond Same-Sex Marriage"); one rolled vinyl banner ("QUIR: Queer Immigrant Rights Project")2"Sex Worker Visions" poster20063QEJ plastic sign3Event fliers3"A New Queer Agenda" poster3"A Time for Revolutionary Change" poster20053"End Eviction Orders!"3"Gay Seniors in New York City" informational poster3Barack Poster3Pastor Martin Niemoller quote poster3Handmade signs and posters remembering individuals who had died, and messages including "Silence = Death" "Queer the Mind" and "Don't Deny It, Stonewall was a Riot."4Planning Calendar (Dry Erase)20125Queers for Economic Justice website

These begin in December 1999 and are fairly complete.Welfare Warriors shelter surveys2007721Board Packet722Mailing lists723Board/Next steps/Merger, etc2010724Board List725Major Donor Party Information726Notes to Financial Statements2009727Board meeting notes and agenda2010728LGBT Health Coalition/board agenda2010729Board members, information, lease contract20042010730Funder briefing, meeting minutes20112013731Financial meetings2012732ARI Development2013733New Funding2012734Meeting notes and information2001-2002735-43Network and Coalition file744Pride Planning meetingMay2001745Meeting notesApril2001746Board nominees, meeting minutes, agendas, and notes2003-20052010747Meeting notesMay-June2001748-49Phone Manual750Board Minutes2009751Somjen suggestions2010752OCT Board Materials753Board meeting minutesFebruary-November2011754-58Funder briefing and notes759Board minutes20072013760Transition761Board plans and policies200781Worker's Comp200782Board Agenda201183-4Nominee forms85QEJ Network86Board policies and agendas200587QEJ Network88Finances and Agendas201089-10Meeting minutes and agendaDecember1999811Facilitator Agenda, site descriptions, LGBT pamphlets and schedules2010812Meeting minutes, agendasJanuary-March2001813-15Reauthorization: Senate meetings816Meeting notes and agendas2003-2004817Reports and minutes818Steering Committee information and ideas2001-2003819Board agendas and information2010-2012820Packing slips and copies of checks136Personnel files, sign-in sheets, and tax documents11
Conditions Governing Access
Access restrictedPersonnel files, sign-in sheets, and tax documents12
Conditions Governing Access
Access restrictedSeries IV. Financial Records
Scope and Contents
Budget materials are in this series, along with miscellaneous files that might rightfully belong in other series, including speeches, conferences, and information on programs and an affordable housing rally.Social Security City Council Hearing2005107Philadelphia Bar Association2005108Conferences, speeches109Domestic Partners in Shelters1010Affordable Housing Rally1011Election Town Hall Meeting20041012Program information2006-20111013CORI/Bookkeepers20101014Landlord dispute20131015N. Cheng20111016Audits2008-20091017-18Mailing list1019Lease20121020Lease and consultant agreement, receipt2010-20111021Budget notes, meeting minutes, tax information2005-20061022NYS Department of Labor20041023Income reports2004-20051024Income and expense reports2004-20051025Budget20041026Budget20061027Audit20041028WWRC Resource Guide1029IRS20031030Sierra admin job description1031Lila interview about LGBT aging1032YWCA/QEJ coalition1033Building meeting20111034Pride at Work20101035Sex worker statements20111036Phone banking document and lists20131037-38QEJ projects1039Black and Pink agreement20121040Faith and Labor Project1041FIERCE/Building incident20111042Whitehouse conference on Housing and Astraea forms20121043Rapoport building work1044Correspondence on blog posts, copies of articles20061045QEJ Network: DYCD1046HIV Ban20071047Series V. Project Files
Scope and Contents
These files document the core work QEJ did, including sharing their insight and knowledge at Creating Change, the annual activist conference hosted by the National LGBT Task Force. There is good documentation of the annual Amazingly Queer Race for Economic Justice, a "scavenger hunt across the city, in which competing teams will raise funds to combat poverty, homelessness and other urban injustices." There are three folders about the working group that wrote Beyond Same-Sex Marriage: A New Strategic Vision For All Our Families and Relationships, and these shed light on how the marriage summit came to be and the behind-the-scenes thinking about it.Pages from meeting brainstorming328Shelter Safety Project329Sheets from a workshop QEJ convened2001330Class and Queer Agenda Making2002331Creating Change Conference and Class Institute docs2004332Welfare Queens333Institute334Corporate Funding2004335Group Feedback2005336The Amazingly Queer Race clues and information2012337Amazing Race for Economic Justice338Corporate Welfare project sheets339Creating Change2005340Institute2003341General C.C. Students, Volunteer, and Intern Program
Scope and Contents
Among the job descriptions and reports about students, volunteers, and interns are information about a QEJ transgender meeting, and notes from a 1999 QEJ network meeting, prior to QEJ's founding.Grant information2008-2011912QEJ trans meeting information913Legal Aid Society2006914NGLTF Consultant Agreements915Summer intern report2012916Volunteer forms917Homelessness forum918Amazing Queer Race sign-in2011919Shelter Facilitator training materials2013920QEJ Supervision921JT Education plan922QEJ Staff Meeting2010923Doyin2011924Caterers925Reina2011926Job descriptions, SEED Report2011927First public work and meeting for QEJ network1999928QEJ supervision report2004930Doyin supervision931Debanuj supervision2006-2007932Shelter organization933Travel/trips2008934Volunteer forms135Series VIII. Publicity
Scope and Contents
The brochures, fliers, booklets, and posters designed to share information about the Shelter Safety Welfare Warriors, the Amazingly Queer Race for Economic Justice, and other projects, as well as articles about QEJ and one folder on QEJ's history. The folder "Why Welfare Reform is a Queer Issue" includes materials from 1996-1999, prior to QEJ's founding. Among the posters are:"Miss Jay Toole: Building for Social Justice," "No Human is Illegal," "Sex Worker Visions," "End Eviction Orders!" "Welfare Made a Difference," handmade signs and posters, and a plastic QEJ sign.Pamphlets, fliers, and booklets71-2Amazingly Queer Race planning book (from binder)201173QEJ Support Group Feedback2004-200774QEJ History75Building a Queer Left2000-200476Amazingly Queer Race folder packet77Why Welfare Reform is a Queer Issue1996-199978Welfare Reform1996-199979City Limits article2001710Speaking About Economic Justice2000711Social Security Panel: Creating Change2005712DASIS Letter2001713Planet Out article2000714QEJ stickers and cards715Welfare Warriors research collaborative716Shelter Safety Welfare Warriors717WWRC Attendance2008718WWRC information2007-2009719Articles20012004720"Welfare Made a Difference" and "Gay City: Vol. 3 No. 319" posters20041"They take you in the morning, they will be coming for us at night" poster1"Tales of the Waria" movie poster1Miss Jay Toole: Building for Social Justice poster1"No Human is Illegal" poster1Council of the City of New York proclamation to QEJ1Two vinyl banners banners ("Queers for Economic Justice," carried in marches and parades; "Beyond Same-Sex Marriage"); one rolled vinyl banner ("QUIR: Queer Immigrant Rights Project")2"Sex Worker Visions" poster20063QEJ plastic sign3Event fliers3"A New Queer Agenda" poster3"A Time for Revolutionary Change" poster20053"End Eviction Orders!"3"Gay Seniors in New York City" informational poster3Barack Poster3Pastor Martin Niemoller quote poster3Handmade signs and posters remembering individuals who had died, and messages including "Silence = Death" "Queer the Mind" and "Don't Deny It, Stonewall was a Riot."4Planning Calendar (Dry Erase)20125Queers for Economic Justice website
